title: Today's AI Brief: Rehumanizing global health care with agentic AI
summary: A look at AI's role in global healthcare and Amazon's AI-generated product images
imageAlt: Image from source article for Rehumanizing global health care with agentic AI
imageCaption: Rehumanizing global health care with agentic AI
imageSourceTitle: Rehumanizing global health care with agentic AI

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's AI brief. We're exploring how AI is making waves in global healthcare and retail. Let's dive in.

First up, the global healthcare sector is under strain, and agentic AI might be a part of the solution. According to a piece in Technology Review, AI can help rehumanize healthcare by taking on more administrative tasks, freeing up professionals to focus on patient care. This comes at a time when the sector is dealing with chronic underinvestment and a surge in demand for services, particularly for aging populations.

In a move that's left some scratching their heads, Amazon is now displaying AI-generated product images within its shopping app. As reported by TechCrunch, these images are based on users' search queries. I mean, who hasn't searched for something and thought, 'You know what would be great? An AI's interpretation of what I'm looking for.' It's an interesting use of AI, to say the least.

My quick read is that AI is slowly but surely seeping into every aspect of our lives, from healthcare to online shopping. While it's exciting to see AI being applied in new ways, it's also a reminder that with great power comes great responsibility – especially when it comes to data boundaries, safety, and trust.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
